1	package graphql
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"crypto/rand"
6		"crypto/sha256"
7		"encoding/hex"
8		"errors"
9		"fmt"
10		"net/http"
11		"net/url"
12		"strings"
13		"sync"
14		"time"
15	
16		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
17		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/evidence"
18		"github.com/vektah/gqlparser/v2/gqlerror"
19	)
20	
21	// DefaultOperatorPromptCapBytes is the fallback prompt-body cap when the
22	// resolver was constructed without an explicit PromptCapBytes value. It
23	// mirrors the inline-prompt cap enforced by the REST /api/agent/run handler
24	// (evidence.DefaultMaxPromptBytes) so the two write surfaces share a limit.
25	const DefaultOperatorPromptCapBytes = evidence.DefaultMaxPromptBytes
26	
27	// CSRFHeaderName is the HTTP header that must carry the server-issued CSRF
28	// token on operatorPromptSubmit calls (FEAT operator-prompts, Story 15).
29	const CSRFHeaderName = "X-CSRF-Token"
30	
31	// OperatorPromptIdempotencyTTL is the window during which duplicate
32	// idempotency keys deduplicate to the original bead.
33	const OperatorPromptIdempotencyTTL = 24 * time.Hour
34	
35	// OperatorPromptEventKind is the BeadEvent.Kind appended to every
36	// operator-prompt bead's audit log on submit, capturing the originating
37	// identity (localhost OR ts-net WhoIs).
38	const OperatorPromptEventKind = "operator_prompt_submit"
39	
40	// OperatorPromptApproveEventKind is the BeadEvent.Kind appended to an
41	// operator-prompt bead's audit log when it transitions proposed → open
42	// (either via operatorPromptApprove or via the auto-approve path on
43	// operatorPromptSubmit).
44	const OperatorPromptApproveEventKind = "operator_prompt_approve"
45	
46	// OperatorPromptCancelEventKind is the BeadEvent.Kind appended to an
47	// operator-prompt bead's audit log when it transitions proposed → cancelled
48	// via operatorPromptCancel.
49	const OperatorPromptCancelEventKind = "operator_prompt_cancel"
50	
51	// OperatorPromptAllowlistLocalhostSentinel is the sentinel allowlist entry
52	// that grants any localhost-kind identity auto-approve / approve rights.
53	// Per the locked Story 15 decision, ts-net identities are never eligible
54	// regardless of the allowlist contents.
55	const OperatorPromptAllowlistLocalhostSentinel = "localhost"
56	
57	// httpRequestKey is the context key used by the server to plumb the
58	// originating *http.Request into GraphQL resolver calls so the resolver can
59	// validate CSRF tokens and capture the originating identity.
60	type httpRequestKey struct{}
61	
62	// WithHTTPRequest returns ctx with r attached for downstream resolver access.
63	// The server's GraphQL HTTP handler must call this before delegating to the
64	// gqlgen handler so resolvers that depend on request headers (CSRF token,
65	// X-Tailscale-User) work correctly.
66	func WithHTTPRequest(ctx context.Context, r *http.Request) context.Context {
67		return context.WithValue(ctx, httpRequestKey{}, r)
68	}
69	
70	// httpRequestFromContext retrieves the *http.Request previously attached via
71	// WithHTTPRequest, or nil if none was set.
72	func httpRequestFromContext(ctx context.Context) *http.Request {
73		if v, ok := ctx.Value(httpRequestKey{}).(*http.Request); ok {
74			return v
75		}
76		return nil
77	}
78	
79	// CSRFTokenStore validates a CSRF token presented on a write request. The
80	// production implementation stores a per-process token issued at server
81	// startup and rotated via an explicit endpoint; tests inject a stub.
82	type CSRFTokenStore interface {
83		Validate(token string) bool
84	}
85	
86	// IdempotencyCache deduplicates operatorPromptSubmit calls by key within
87	// OperatorPromptIdempotencyTTL. Lookup returns the original bead ID stored
88	// under key (and true) when the key was previously seen and has not expired,
89	// or "", false otherwise. Store records key -> beadID at submit time.
90	type IdempotencyCache interface {
91		Lookup(key string) (string, bool)
92		Store(key, beadID string)
93	}
94	
95	// MemoryIdempotencyCache is a process-local in-memory IdempotencyCache with
96	// TTL-based eviction. It is safe for concurrent use.
97	type MemoryIdempotencyCache struct {
98		mu  sync.Mutex
99		now func() time.Time
100		ttl time.Duration
101		m   map[string]idempotencyEntry
102	}
103	
104	type idempotencyEntry struct {
105		beadID    string
106		expiresAt time.Time
107	}
108	
109	// NewMemoryIdempotencyCache constructs a cache with the operator-prompt
110	// default 24h TTL and the real wall clock.
111	func NewMemoryIdempotencyCache() *MemoryIdempotencyCache {
112		return &MemoryIdempotencyCache{
113			now: time.Now,
114			ttl: OperatorPromptIdempotencyTTL,
115			m:   make(map[string]idempotencyEntry),
116		}
117	}
118	
119	// Lookup returns the bead ID associated with key when present and unexpired.
120	func (c *MemoryIdempotencyCache) Lookup(key string) (string, bool) {
121		if key == "" {
122			return "", false
123		}
124		c.mu.Lock()
125		defer c.mu.Unlock()
126		e, ok := c.m[key]
127		if !ok {
128			return "", false
129		}
130		if c.now().After(e.expiresAt) {
131			delete(c.m, key)
132			return "", false
133		}
134		return e.beadID, true
135	}
136	
137	// Store records key -> beadID with the configured TTL window.
138	func (c *MemoryIdempotencyCache) Store(key, beadID string) {
139		if key == "" {
140			return
141		}
142		c.mu.Lock()
143		defer c.mu.Unlock()
144		c.m[key] = idempotencyEntry{beadID: beadID, expiresAt: c.now().Add(c.ttl)}
145	}
146	
147	// StaticCSRFTokenStore is a single-secret CSRFTokenStore — the token is
148	// generated once at server startup and validated by constant-time compare.
149	type StaticCSRFTokenStore struct {
150		token string
151	}
152	
153	// NewStaticCSRFTokenStore returns a store seeded with a freshly-generated
154	// 32-byte random token.
155	func NewStaticCSRFTokenStore() (*StaticCSRFTokenStore, error) {
156		var buf [32]byte
157		if _, err := rand.Read(buf[:]); err != nil {
158			return nil, fmt.Errorf("csrf: generate token: %w", err)
159		}
160		return &StaticCSRFTokenStore{token: hex.EncodeToString(buf[:])}, nil
161	}
162	
163	// NewStaticCSRFTokenStoreWithToken constructs a store using the supplied
164	// token (used by tests so a deterministic header value can be sent).
165	func NewStaticCSRFTokenStoreWithToken(token string) *StaticCSRFTokenStore {
166		return &StaticCSRFTokenStore{token: token}
167	}
168	
169	// Token returns the stored CSRF token (used by the server to expose it via a
170	// dedicated endpoint or embed it in served HTML).
171	func (s *StaticCSRFTokenStore) Token() string { return s.token }
172	
173	// Validate compares presented to the stored token using a length-equal
174	// constant-time check.
175	func (s *StaticCSRFTokenStore) Validate(presented string) bool {
176		if presented == "" || len(presented) != len(s.token) {
177			return false
178		}
179		var diff byte
180		for i := 0; i < len(s.token); i++ {
181			diff |= s.token[i] ^ presented[i]
182		}
183		return diff == 0
184	}
185	
186	// errCSRF is returned by OperatorPromptSubmit when the CSRF check fails.
187	// The HTTP transport for the GraphQL endpoint surfaces this as a 403; the
188	// resolver itself only emits the error.
189	var errCSRF = errors.New("operator-prompts: CSRF token missing or invalid (status: 403)")
190	
191	// errCrossOrigin is returned by OperatorPromptSubmit when the request carries
192	// an Origin header whose host portion does not match the request Host. This
193	// blocks browser cross-origin POSTs even from a trusted-loopback peer (CSRF
194	// defense in depth alongside the per-session token).
195	var errCrossOrigin = errors.New("operator-prompts: cross-origin request rejected (status: 403)")
196	
197	// promptCapError builds the structured cap-error envelope returned by
198	// operatorPromptSubmit when the prompt body exceeds the configured cap. The
199	// envelope is surfaced inside `errors[].extensions` so the SvelteKit
200	// frontend can show observed/cap bytes without parsing the message string.
201	func promptCapError(observed, cap int) error {
202		return &gqlerror.Error{
203			Message: fmt.Sprintf(
204				"operator-prompts: prompt body exceeds cap: observed %d bytes, cap %d bytes (status: 400)",
205				observed, cap,
206			),
207			Extensions: map[string]any{
208				"code":           "PROMPT_TOO_LARGE",
209				"status":         400,
210				"observed_bytes": observed,
211				"cap_bytes":      cap,
212				"config_hint":    ".ddx/config.yaml:evidence_caps.max_prompt_bytes",
213			},
214		}
215	}
216	
217	// errAutoApproveDenied is returned by OperatorPromptApprove (and is the
218	// reason auto-approve gets demoted to a plain proposed bead inside
219	// OperatorPromptSubmit) when the caller's identity is not on the per-project
220	// allowlist. ts-net identities always hit this path regardless of allowlist
221	// contents (locked Story 15 decision: ts-net peers may not blanket
222	// auto-approve operator prompts).
223	var errAutoApproveDenied = errors.New("operator-prompts: identity not on per-project auto-approve allowlist (status: 403)")
224	
225	// errSubmitDenied is returned by OperatorPromptSubmit when the caller is a
226	// ts-net peer whose identity is not on the per-project allowlist
227	// (web.operator_prompt.allow_identities). Localhost callers are always
228	// allowed to submit because they have already cleared the requireTrusted
229	// loopback gate; ts-net peers carry only network-layer trust, so the
230	// per-project allowlist is the authorization step. Empty allowlist →
231	// every ts-net peer is rejected (the locked Story 15 default that yields a
232	// "ts-net peers see read-only UI" outcome).
233	var errSubmitDenied = errors.New("operator-prompts: ts-net identity not on per-project allow_identities allowlist (status: 403)")
234	
235	// OperatorPromptSubmit implements the operatorPromptSubmit mutation: it
236	// validates the CSRF token from the request header, deduplicates within a
237	// 24h window by idempotency key, and (on first submission) creates a new
238	// operator-prompt bead in the proposed status with an audit event capturing
239	// the originating identity (localhost user OR ts-net WhoIs).
240	func (r *mutationResolver) OperatorPromptSubmit(ctx context.Context, input OperatorPromptSubmitInput) (*OperatorPromptSubmitResult, error) {
241		if r.workingDir(ctx) == "" {
242			return nil, fmt.Errorf("working directory not configured")
243		}
244		if strings.TrimSpace(input.Prompt) == "" {
245			return nil, fmt.Errorf("operator-prompts: prompt is required")
246		}
247		if strings.TrimSpace(input.IdempotencyKey) == "" {
248			return nil, fmt.Errorf("operator-prompts: idempotency key is required")
249		}
250	
251		httpReq := httpRequestFromContext(ctx)
252		if httpReq == nil {
253			// No request context means we cannot enforce CSRF, which is the
254			// whole point of the gate. Refuse rather than fail open.
255			return nil, errCSRF
256		}
257		if err := validateSameOrigin(httpReq); err != nil {
258			return nil, err
259		}
260		presented := httpReq.Header.Get(CSRFHeaderName)
261		if r.CSRFTokens == nil || !r.CSRFTokens.Validate(presented) {
262			return nil, errCSRF
263		}
264	
265		identity := operatorPromptIdentity(httpReq)
266		if !r.canSubmit(identity) {
267			return nil, errSubmitDenied
268		}
269	
270		cap := r.PromptCapBytes
271		if cap <= 0 {
272			cap = DefaultOperatorPromptCapBytes
273		}
274		if observed := len(input.Prompt); observed > cap {
275			return nil, promptCapError(observed, cap)
276		}
277	
278		cache := r.OperatorPromptIdempotency
279		if cache == nil {
280			return nil, fmt.Errorf("operator-prompts: idempotency cache not configured")
281		}
282	
283		store := r.beadStore(ctx)
284		if id, ok := cache.Lookup(input.IdempotencyKey); ok {
285			existing, err := store.Get(id)
286			if err == nil {
287				return &OperatorPromptSubmitResult{
288					Bead:         beadModelFromBead(existing),
289					Deduplicated: true,
290				}, nil
291			}
292			// Cached ID points to a missing bead — fall through and create a
293			// fresh bead, overwriting the stale entry below.
294		}
295	
296		tier := bead.DefaultPriority
297		if input.Tier != nil {
298			tier = *input.Tier
299		}
300		b := bead.NewOperatorPromptBead(input.Prompt, tier)
301		if err := store.Create(b); err != nil {
302			return nil, err
303		}
304	
305		originNodeID := httpReq.Header.Get("X-Tailscale-Node")
306		if originNodeID == "" {
307			// Loopback callers do not carry an X-Tailscale-Node header; the
308			// origin and the receiving node are the same machine, so record the
309			// receiving node ID for symmetry.
310			originNodeID = r.NodeID
311		}
312		buildSHA := r.BuildSHA
313		if buildSHA == "" {
314			buildSHA = "unknown"
315		}
316		approvalMode := "manual"
317		if input.AutoApprove != nil && *input.AutoApprove {
318			approvalMode = "auto-requested"
319		}
320		requestID := httpReq.Header.Get("X-Request-Id")
321		if requestID == "" {
322			requestID = newRequestID()
323		}
324		promptHash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(input.Prompt))
325		promptSHA := hex.EncodeToString(promptHash[:])
326		auditBody := fmt.Sprintf(
327			"identity=%s remote_addr=%s tailscale_user=%s tailscale_node=%s "+
328				"origin_node_id=%s receiving_node_id=%s build_sha=%s "+
329				"approval_mode=%s request_id=%s prompt_sha256=%s "+
330				"idempotency_key=%s",
331			identity.kind,
332			httpReq.RemoteAddr,
333			httpReq.Header.Get("X-Tailscale-User"),
334			httpReq.Header.Get("X-Tailscale-Node"),
335			originNodeID,
336			r.NodeID,
337			buildSHA,
338			approvalMode,
339			requestID,
340			promptSHA,
341			input.IdempotencyKey,
342		)
343		if err := store.AppendEvent(b.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
344			Kind:    OperatorPromptEventKind,
345			Summary: "operator prompt submitted",
346			Body:    auditBody,
347			Actor:   identity.actor,
348			Source:  "graphql:operatorPromptSubmit",
349		}); err != nil {
350			return nil, fmt.Errorf("operator-prompts: append audit event: %w", err)
351		}
352	
353		cache.Store(input.IdempotencyKey, b.ID)
354	
355		// Auto-approve path: only when the caller asked for it AND the identity
356		// is on the per-project allowlist. ts-net identities never qualify
357		// (locked Story 15 decision). On denial we leave the bead in proposed
358		// and return autoApproved=false; the caller can still issue a manual
359		// operatorPromptApprove later from a permitted identity.
360		autoApproved := false
361		if input.AutoApprove != nil && *input.AutoApprove {
362			if r.canAutoApprove(identity) {
363				if err := store.Update(b.ID, func(updated *bead.Bead) {
364					updated.Status = bead.StatusOpen
365				}); err != nil {
366					return nil, fmt.Errorf("operator-prompts: auto-approve update: %w", err)
367				}
368				if err := store.AppendEvent(b.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
369					Kind:    OperatorPromptApproveEventKind,
370					Summary: "operator prompt auto-approved on submit",
371					Body:    fmt.Sprintf("identity=%s actor=%s", identity.kind, identity.actor),
372					Actor:   identity.actor,
373					Source:  "graphql:operatorPromptSubmit",
374				}); err != nil {
375					return nil, fmt.Errorf("operator-prompts: append auto-approve event: %w", err)
376				}
377				autoApproved = true
378				r.signalExecuteLoopWake()
379			}
380		}
381	
382		// Re-read so the audit event materializes in the returned snapshot.
383		persisted, err := store.Get(b.ID)
384		if err != nil {
385			return nil, err
386		}
387		return &OperatorPromptSubmitResult{
388			Bead:         beadModelFromBead(persisted),
389			Deduplicated: false,
390			AutoApproved: autoApproved,
391		}, nil
392	}
393	
394	// OperatorPromptApprove implements the operatorPromptApprove mutation:
395	// validates CSRF, verifies the bead is a proposed operator-prompt, enforces
396	// the per-project allowlist (ts-net identities are never eligible per
397	// locked decision), transitions proposed → open, and appends an audit event
398	// recording the approver.
399	func (r *mutationResolver) OperatorPromptApprove(ctx context.Context, id string) (*OperatorPromptApproveResult, error) {
400		httpReq, ident, err := r.requireOperatorPromptCSRF(ctx)
401		if err != nil {
402			return nil, err
403		}
404		if !r.canAutoApprove(ident) {
405			return nil, errAutoApproveDenied
406		}
407	
408		store := r.beadStore(ctx)
409		existing, err := store.Get(id)
410		if err != nil {
411			return nil, err
412		}
413		if existing.IssueType != bead.IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
414			return nil, fmt.Errorf("operator-prompts: bead %s is not an operator-prompt (issue_type=%q)", id, existing.IssueType)
415		}
416		if existing.Status != bead.StatusProposed {
417			return nil, fmt.Errorf("operator-prompts: bead %s is not in proposed status (got %q); cannot approve", id, existing.Status)
418		}
419		if err := store.Update(id, func(b *bead.Bead) {
420			b.Status = bead.StatusOpen
421		}); err != nil {
422			return nil, err
423		}
424		if err := store.AppendEvent(id, bead.BeadEvent{
425			Kind:    OperatorPromptApproveEventKind,
426			Summary: "operator prompt approved",
427			Body:    fmt.Sprintf("identity=%s actor=%s remote_addr=%s", ident.kind, ident.actor, httpReq.RemoteAddr),
428			Actor:   ident.actor,
429			Source:  "graphql:operatorPromptApprove",
430		}); err != nil {
431			return nil, fmt.Errorf("operator-prompts: append approve audit event: %w", err)
432		}
433		persisted, err := store.Get(id)
434		if err != nil {
435			return nil, err
436		}
437		r.signalExecuteLoopWake()
438		return &OperatorPromptApproveResult{Bead: beadModelFromBead(persisted)}, nil
439	}
440	
441	// signalExecuteLoopWake asks any running execute-loop worker bound to this
442	// project to skip the current idle-poll sleep and re-scan the ready queue.
443	// Called immediately after a successful proposed → open transition (manual
444	// approve or auto-approve on submit) so a freshly-eligible operator-prompt
445	// bead is claimed without waiting a full PollInterval. No-op when the
446	// waker is unset (e.g. test fixture without a WorkerManager).
447	func (r *Resolver) signalExecuteLoopWake() {
448		if r.ExecuteLoopWaker == nil || r.WorkingDir == "" {
449			return
450		}
451		r.ExecuteLoopWaker.WakeProject(r.WorkingDir)
452	}
453	
454	// OperatorPromptCancel implements the operatorPromptCancel mutation:
455	// validates CSRF, verifies the bead is a proposed operator-prompt,
456	// transitions proposed → cancelled, and appends an audit event recording
457	// the canceller. Cancellation is open to any trusted identity (the
458	// allowlist gates auto-approval, not rejection) so an operator who
459	// regrets a submission can withdraw it without first being added to the
460	// approval allowlist.
461	func (r *mutationResolver) OperatorPromptCancel(ctx context.Context, id string) (*OperatorPromptCancelResult, error) {
462		httpReq, ident, err := r.requireOperatorPromptCSRF(ctx)
463		if err != nil {
464			return nil, err
465		}
466	
467		store := r.beadStore(ctx)
468		existing, err := store.Get(id)
469		if err != nil {
470			return nil, err
471		}
472		if existing.IssueType != bead.IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
473			return nil, fmt.Errorf("operator-prompts: bead %s is not an operator-prompt (issue_type=%q)", id, existing.IssueType)
474		}
475		if existing.Status != bead.StatusProposed {
476			return nil, fmt.Errorf("operator-prompts: bead %s is not in proposed status (got %q); cannot cancel", id, existing.Status)
477		}
478		if err := store.Update(id, func(b *bead.Bead) {
479			b.Status = bead.StatusCancelled
480		}); err != nil {
481			return nil, err
482		}
483		if err := store.AppendEvent(id, bead.BeadEvent{
484			Kind:    OperatorPromptCancelEventKind,
485			Summary: "operator prompt cancelled",
486			Body:    fmt.Sprintf("identity=%s actor=%s remote_addr=%s", ident.kind, ident.actor, httpReq.RemoteAddr),
487			Actor:   ident.actor,
488			Source:  "graphql:operatorPromptCancel",
489		}); err != nil {
490			return nil, fmt.Errorf("operator-prompts: append cancel audit event: %w", err)
491		}
492		persisted, err := store.Get(id)
493		if err != nil {
494			return nil, err
495		}
496		return &OperatorPromptCancelResult{Bead: beadModelFromBead(persisted)}, nil
497	}
498	
499	// requireOperatorPromptCSRF is the shared preamble for the approve and
500	// cancel mutations. It pulls the originating *http.Request from context
501	// (the server's GraphQL handler installs it via WithHTTPRequest), enforces
502	// the CSRF token check against the configured store, and returns the
503	// classified identity.
504	func (r *mutationResolver) requireOperatorPromptCSRF(ctx context.Context) (*http.Request, operatorPromptIdentityInfo, error) {
505		if r.workingDir(ctx) == "" {
506			return nil, operatorPromptIdentityInfo{}, fmt.Errorf("working directory not configured")
507		}
508		httpReq := httpRequestFromContext(ctx)
509		if httpReq == nil {
510			return nil, operatorPromptIdentityInfo{}, errCSRF
511		}
512		presented := httpReq.Header.Get(CSRFHeaderName)
513		if r.CSRFTokens == nil || !r.CSRFTokens.Validate(presented) {
514			return nil, operatorPromptIdentityInfo{}, errCSRF
515		}
516		return httpReq, operatorPromptIdentity(httpReq), nil
517	}
518	
519	// canSubmit reports whether ident is authorized to submit operator prompts
520	// for this project. Localhost identities are always allowed (they have
521	// already cleared the requireTrusted loopback gate, which is the project's
522	// strongest trust signal). ts-net identities must appear in the per-project
523	// OperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist (the same list that gates approval —
524	// per Story 15 the project's "allow_identities" config is one allowlist
525	// serving both submit-by-tsnet and approve-by-localhost). An empty list
526	// rejects every ts-net peer, yielding the documented "ts-net peers see
527	// read-only UI" default.
528	func (r *Resolver) canSubmit(ident operatorPromptIdentityInfo) bool {
529		if ident.kind == "localhost" {
530			return true
531		}
532		if ident.kind != "tsnet" {
533			return false
534		}
535		for _, entry := range r.OperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist {
536			if entry == ident.actor {
537				return true
538			}
539		}
540		return false
541	}
542	
543	// canAutoApprove reports whether ident is authorized to approve operator
544	// prompts under the resolver's allowlist. ts-net identities are denied
545	// unconditionally (locked Story 15 decision). For localhost identities,
546	// the literal sentinel "localhost" matches any actor; otherwise the
547	// allowlist entry must equal the identity's actor string.
548	func (r *Resolver) canAutoApprove(ident operatorPromptIdentityInfo) bool {
549		if ident.kind != "localhost" {
550			return false
551		}
552		if len(r.OperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist) == 0 {
553			return false
554		}
555		for _, entry := range r.OperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist {
556			if entry == OperatorPromptAllowlistLocalhostSentinel {
557				return true
558			}
559			if entry == ident.actor {
560				return true
561			}
562		}
563		return false
564	}
565	
566	type operatorPromptIdentityInfo struct {
567		kind  string // "tsnet" or "localhost"
568		actor string
569	}
570	
571	// operatorPromptIdentity classifies the originating identity for audit
572	// logging. ts-net peers are identified by the X-Tailscale-User header
573	// injected by tsnetMiddleware; loopback callers fall back to a
574	// "localhost:<remote-addr>" actor string. A bare "anonymous" actor is used
575	// only when neither signal is present (which should not occur because
576	// requireTrusted gates the GraphQL endpoint).
577	func operatorPromptIdentity(r *http.Request) operatorPromptIdentityInfo {
578		if user := r.Header.Get("X-Tailscale-User"); user != "" {
579			return operatorPromptIdentityInfo{kind: "tsnet", actor: user}
580		}
581		if r.RemoteAddr != "" {
582			return operatorPromptIdentityInfo{kind: "localhost", actor: "localhost:" + r.RemoteAddr}
583		}
584		return operatorPromptIdentityInfo{kind: "unknown", actor: "anonymous"}
585	}
586	
587	// validateSameOrigin enforces strict Origin/Host equality on prompt-driven
588	// mutations. When the request carries an Origin header (every browser POST
589	// does), the host portion must match the request Host — this rejects
590	// browser cross-origin POSTs even from a trusted-loopback peer (defense in
591	// depth alongside the per-session CSRF token). Non-browser clients (curl,
592	// the SvelteKit fetch wrapper running same-origin) either omit Origin or
593	// emit an Origin matching Host, so they pass through unchanged.
594	func validateSameOrigin(r *http.Request) error {
595		origin := r.Header.Get("Origin")
596		if origin == "" {
597			// No Origin -> not a browser cross-origin POST. CSRF token is the
598			// remaining gate.
599			return nil
600		}
601		parsed, err := url.Parse(origin)
602		if err != nil || parsed.Host == "" {
603			return errCrossOrigin
604		}
605		if !sameHost(parsed.Host, r.Host) {
606			return errCrossOrigin
607		}
608		return nil
609	}
610	
611	// sameHost compares two host[:port] strings tolerating an absent port (a
612	// browser may omit :443 from Origin while the request Host carries it, or
613	// vice versa). The comparison is case-insensitive on the host component.
614	func sameHost(a, b string) bool {
615		ah, _ := splitHostPort(a)
616		bh, _ := splitHostPort(b)
617		if !strings.EqualFold(ah, bh) {
618			return false
619		}
620		// Reject when both sides carry a port and they disagree; tolerate the
621		// "one side omits the port" case (default-port elision by the browser).
622		_, ap := splitHostPort(a)
623		_, bp := splitHostPort(b)
624		if ap != "" && bp != "" && ap != bp {
625			return false
626		}
627		return true
628	}
629	
630	func splitHostPort(hp string) (string, string) {
631		if i := strings.LastIndex(hp, ":"); i > 0 && !strings.Contains(hp[i:], "]") {
632			return hp[:i], hp[i+1:]
633		}
634		return hp, ""
635	}
636	
637	// newRequestID returns a 16-byte hex random string used as the request ID
638	// recorded on the audit event when the caller does not supply X-Request-Id.
639	func newRequestID() string {
640		var buf [16]byte
641		if _, err := rand.Read(buf[:]); err != nil {
642			return "no-request-id"
643		}
644		return hex.EncodeToString(buf[:])
645	}
646

data.text: All ACs are already satisfied at the base revision. Implementation lives in `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt.go` with comprehensive tests in `resolver_operator_prompt_lifecycle_test.go`. Writing a no_changes rationale with a verification command.

input.content:
Bead ddx-263ad347 — operatorPromptApprove/Cancel + per-project allowlist + auto-approve flag

All four acceptance criteria are already satisfied at the base revision (bb3b5883). No source change is required.

AC #1 — Approve/cancel mutations exist:
  - cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql declares operatorPromptApprove and operatorPromptCancel.
  - Resolvers: cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt.go
      OperatorPromptApprove (line 399)
      OperatorPromptCancel  (line 461)

AC #2 — Per-project allowlist enforced:
  - Resolver.OperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist is the per-project allowlist.
  - canSubmit (line 528) gates ts-net submitters; canAutoApprove (line 548) gates approve.
  - Empty allowlist denies approval and ts-net submission (errSubmitDenied / errAutoApproveDenied).
  - Tests:
      TestOperatorPromptApprove_DeniedEmptyAllowlist
      TestOperatorPromptSubmit_TsnetNotInAllowlistRejected
      TestOperatorPromptSubmit_TsnetOnAllowlistAccepted

AC #3 — Auto-approve restricted to configured-localhost:
  - canAutoApprove returns false for any non-localhost identity (line 549), regardless of allowlist contents.
  - OperatorPromptAllowlistLocalhostSentinel ("localhost") is the opt-in sentinel.
  - Tests:
      TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveAllowedForLocalhost  (localhost+sentinel → autoApproved=true, status=open)
      TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveDeniedForTsnet       (ts-net never auto-approves even when on allowlist)

AC #4 — Tests cover happy + denied paths:
  - Happy: TestOperatorPromptApprove_Happy, TestOperatorPromptCancel_Happy, TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveAllowedForLocalhost, TestOperatorPromptSubmit_TsnetOnAllowlistAccepted
  - Denied: TestOperatorPromptApprove_DeniedEmptyAllowlist, TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApproveDeniedForTsnet, TestOperatorPromptSubmit_TsnetNotInAllowlistRejected, TestOperatorPromptApprove_CSRFRequired, TestOperatorPromptCancel_CSRFRequired, TestOperatorPromptApprove_RejectsNonProposed, TestOperatorPromptCancel_RejectsNonProposed

Manual approve = proposed → ready: OperatorPromptApprove sets b.Status = bead.StatusOpen (the "ready" state for execute-loop pickup; the codebase uses StatusOpen as the ready bucket — see execute_bead_loop.go and the wake tests).

Prior REVIEW:BLOCK notes on this bead complained that the diff contained only execution metadata. That observation is correct — but the source change those reviewers were looking for had already landed before bb3b5883. The bead's contract is satisfied by the existing code; another implementation pass would be duplicate or churn.

verification_command: cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/ -run 'OperatorPrompt' -count=1

data.text:
All four ACs are already satisfied at base-rev `bb3b5883`:
- Approve/cancel mutations: `resolver_operator_prompt.go:399, 461`
- Per-project allowlist: `Resolver.OperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist` enforced via `canSubmit`/`canAutoApprove`
- Auto-approve restricted to localhost identity (ts-net always denied per locked Story 15)
- Tests: 11 lifecycle tests + security/wake tests, all passing

Wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` with `verification_command: cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/ -run 'OperatorPrompt' -count=1` (verified passing locally).
